skill.name: tailwind
skill.description: Tailwind CSS v4.2 browser-runtime patterns for HyperFrames compositions. Use when scaffolding or editing projects created with `hyperframes init --tailwind`, writing Tailwind utility classes in composition HTML, adding CSS-first Tailwind v4 theme tokens, debugging v3 vs v4 syntax, or deciding when to compile Tailwind to CSS instead of using the browser runtime.
skill.summary: Tailwind CSS v4.2 browser-runtime patterns for HyperFrames video compositions. Guides developers on CSS-first theme tokens, safe utility class patterns, and deterministic video frame output.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: All 54 external_commands findings are false positives -- they are markdown backtick formatting in a documentation-only SKILL.md file, not Ruby shell execution. The 2 network findings are citations to official tailwindcss.com documentation URLs. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' finding is a misidentification of the CSS oklch() color function. The 'System reconnaissance' findings match CSS class name examples and legitimate advice text. This skill contains no executable code and presents no security risk.

content.user_title: Use Tailwind CSS v4 with HyperFrames video compositions
content.value_statement: HyperFrames video compositions that use Tailwind CSS need the correct v4 browser-runtime patterns instead of v3 configuration files. This skill provides the exact rules, theme tokens, and safe utility class patterns for deterministic render-ready video frames with Tailwind CSS v4.2.
actual_capabilities: Generate Tailwind v4 CSS-first theme tokens using @theme and @utility directives
Write deterministic Tailwind utility classes for HyperFrames video compositions
Debug v3 versus v4 syntax differences in browser-runtime projects
Apply video-safe CSS patterns with stable dimensions and transforms
Advise on when to compile Tailwind to CSS versus using the browser runtime
limitations: Cannot compile or build Tailwind stylesheets outside the HyperFrames context
Cannot modify Tailwind v3 configuration used in HyperFrames Studio
Does not cover PostCSS, CLI builds, or Tailwind v3 plugin APIs
Cannot debug missing styles caused by runtime timing issues in the browser

use_cases.title: Scaffold a new Tailwind-enabled video composition
use_cases.description: A developer creating a new HyperFrames project with hyperframes init --tailwind follows the correct v4 browser-runtime setup with pinned script version and readiness shim.

use_cases.title: Migrate v3 Tailwind patterns to v4 for video rendering
use_cases.description: A content creator moving from Tailwind v3 config-based setup to v4 CSS-first browser runtime replaces @tailwind directives, tailwind.config.js, and dynamic class patterns.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators upgrading from Tailwind v3

use_cases.title: Debug missing styles in rendered video output
use_cases.description: A video producer whose rendered frames show missing or incorrect Tailwind styles uses the debug checklist to verify script version, readiness shim, and correct utility class usage.

prompt_templates.title: Set up Tailwind v4 theme tokens
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a Tailwind v4 CSS-first theme with custom brand colors and fonts for a HyperFrames composition. Use @theme in a text/tailwindcss style block. Set --color-brand to oklch(0.62 0.18 265) and --font-display to Inter.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginning a new composition project with brand colors

prompt_templates.title: Style a video title card with Tailwind
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a fullscreen title card for a 1280x720 HyperFrames video composition. Use Tailwind v4 utility classes. Center a heading and subtitle vertically and horizontally. Use a dark background with cyan accent colors and large responsive typography.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building the opening scene of a video

prompt_templates.title: Debug Tailwind rendering issues
prompt_templates.prompt: A HyperFrames composition shows missing styles in rendered video output but looks correct in preview. The project was started with --tailwind. Walk through each step of the debug checklist: verify the init flag, check the script URL points to @tailwindcss/browser@4.2.4, confirm window.__tailwindReady resolves, replace @tailwind directives, and move config tokens to @theme.
prompt_templates.scenario: Troubleshooting missing styles in rendered output

prompt_templates.title: Replace dynamic class names with safe alternatives
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this HyperFrames composition to avoid dynamically assembled class names. The current code uses element.className with template literal inside a seek handler. Replace with a data attribute strategy that uses complete static Tailwind classes and CSS data-attribute selectors.
prompt_templates.scenario: Fixing non-deterministic class generation for frame-accurate capture

output_examples.input: Add Tailwind styling to a HyperFrames scene that shows a product name and tagline centered on screen.
output: A centered layout using grid and place-items-center utility classes on a fullscreen section.
A dark background with light text for high contrast video readability.
A styled heading using large font size and weight with a cyan accent subtitle.

output_examples.input: How do I use custom colors in my Tailwind HyperFrames project?
output: Define colors in an @theme block inside a text/tailwindcss style tag.
Use the CSS-first custom property syntax like --color-brand for brand colors.
Apply them in HTML with bg-brand or text-brand utility classes directly.

best_practices: Use @theme and @utility CSS directives in text/tailwindcss style blocks instead of JavaScript config files for all theme customization.
Keep all render-critical class names as complete static strings in HTML or CSS data-attribute selectors to ensure the browser runtime captures them before frame rendering.
Use transforms and opacity for animated properties in video compositions. Keep Tailwind responsible for layout and visual style only.
anti_patterns: Do not build class names dynamically with JavaScript concatenation at seek time. The browser runtime may not detect them before frame capture.
Do not add a tailwind.config.js file for v4 browser-runtime compositions. Use @theme in CSS for all theme tokens.
Do not rely on hover, focus, or viewport variants for content that must appear in every rendered frame.

faq.question: What version of Tailwind CSS does HyperFrames use?
faq.answer: HyperFrames uses Tailwind v4.2 browser runtime pinned to @tailwindcss/browser@4.2.4. This is different from Tailwind v3 which uses PostCSS and tailwind.config.js.

faq.question: Can I use tailwind.config.js with the browser runtime?
faq.answer: No. The browser runtime uses CSS-first configuration with @theme and @utility directives. For compiled builds you can use @config to load a JavaScript config, but this is not recommended.

faq.question: How does HyperFrames know when Tailwind styles are ready?
faq.answer: HyperFrames waits for the global window.__tailwindReady flag. The Tailwind browser runtime sets this flag when it finishes scanning the document and generating CSS.

faq.question: Why do my Tailwind styles look correct in preview but not in the rendered video?
faq.answer: This usually means the browser runtime has not finished before frame capture. Check that window.__tailwindReady resolves, the pinned script is loaded, and all classes are static strings visible in the initial HTML.

faq.question: Should I compile Tailwind to CSS instead of using the browser runtime?
faq.answer: For offline, locked-down, or production-stable renders, compiling Tailwind to CSS and including the stylesheet directly is more reliable. The browser runtime is best for development and draft renders.

faq.question: Does this skill work with HyperFrames Studio?
faq.answer: No. HyperFrames Studio uses Tailwind v3 internally with PostCSS, tailwind.config.js, and @tailwind directives. This skill is for CLI-generated projects created with the --tailwind flag.
